_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 NC statewide executives (Council of State). All incumbents web-verified in office. State tier. Evidence-first: TRUE only where documented-aligned, FALSE only where documented-contrary; all unspecified applicable cells reset to null.

profile.confidence_note: Sitting NC State Treasurer (R), in office since 2025-01-01. Ran a single-issue finance campaign; declined the NC Family Policy Council values survey. Only one rubric-relevant position is documented (anti-ESG / fiduciary-neutral pension investment); all other cells null for lack of evidence — do NOT infer from party.

brad-briner.notes_set: Web-verified sitting NC State Treasurer (R), elected 2024-11-05, took office 2025-01-01; former investment manager. DOCUMENTED: explicit anti-ESG stance ('the ESG crowd has driven a lemming-like abandonment of sectors like traditional energy') and, as Treasurer in 2025, rejected the 'Break the Bonds NC' BDS/divestment campaign, citing fiduciary duty ('we're in the business of making our state wealthier'). NO documented position found on life, marriage, family, Christian liberty, elections, justice, federalism, immigration, or 2A; he declined the NC Family survey and Ballotpedia listed no issue-group endorsements. Thin record by design.

4.note: Explicit anti-ESG campaign statement criticizing 'the ESG crowd'; as Treasurer refused activist BDS/divestment pressure, keeping pension investment de-politicized. HARD.

profile.confidence_note: Sitting NC State Auditor (R), in office since 2025-01-01. Strongest documented record is government efficiency/anti-waste (DAVE Act, NC's state-level DOGE) and opposing overspending. Election-integrity and anti-illegal-immigration positions are documented as rhetoric but do not map cleanly to the specific rubric cells; nulled rather than over-read.

dave-boliek.notes_set: Web-verified sitting NC State Auditor (R), elected 2024-11-05, took office 2025-01-01; former UNC Board of Trustees chair, former assistant DA. DOCUMENTED: champions the DAVE Act (Division of Accountability, Value & Efficiency — state DOGE), launched DMV/Helene waste audits, eliminated a $100M deficit at UNC, and stated 'both political parties at the federal level have spent entirely too much money' — a documented anti-deficit-spending position (scored). Context (not cleanly scoreable to a cell): as UNC chair opposed CRT/the 1619 Project and voted against the Hannah-Jones tenure (higher-ed, not K-12, so the K-12 curriculum cell is nulled); campaigned to add an Office of Election Integrity, audit voting-machine source code, and scrub voter rolls; criticized illegal aliens obtaining state services and promised a regulatory 'wall' around welfare agencies. Trump-endorsed (Aug 2024). No documented position on marriage, 2A, or specific election/immigration rubric mechanisms.

2.note: Champions the DAVE Act (state-level DOGE) and stated 'both political parties at the federal level have spent entirely too much money'; documented anti-deficit-spending / anti-waste record (eliminated $100M UNC deficit). HARD on opposing deficit spending.

profile.confidence_note: Sitting NC Secretary of State (D), in office since 1997, re-elected 2024. Substantive issue record is HARD but largely from her 2010 US Senate campaign; current footprint is administrative. EMILY's List endorsement is current (2024). Documented liberal positions on life, marriage, and immigration; no cross-party (conservative) deviation found.

elaine-marshall.notes_set: Web-verified sitting NC Secretary of State (D), in office since 1997 (longest-serving), re-elected 2024-11-05. NC SoS runs the business/securities/notary registry — NOT elections (that is the State Board of Elections). DOCUMENTED liberal positions (HARD, chiefly from her 2010 US Senate run via OnTheIssues): 'protect reproductive choice'; opposed the Federal Marriage Amendment and DOMA, 'strong supporter of LGBT rights', backed ENDA and DADT repeal; on immigration 'don't send most illegal immigrants back — deport criminal immigrants, but don't send back others.' Endorsed by EMILY's List in 2010/2012/2020 AND 2024 (current) — an abortion-industry-aligned PAC. NO documented position found on transgender/biological sex, Christian liberty, federalism, or guns (NRA rated her '?'); those cells nulled. No conservative deviation surfaced.

0.note: Documented pro-choice ('protect reproductive choice'; women make their own reproductive decisions) — contrary to affirming life from conception. HARD (2010).

1.note: Advocates abortion rights, the opposite of abortion abolition. HARD.

4.note: Endorsed/backed by EMILY's List repeatedly incl. 2024 (current) — abortion-industry-aligned PAC. HARD.

0.note: Opposed the Federal Marriage Amendment and DOMA; 'strong supporter of LGBT rights' — contrary to man-woman-only marriage. HARD (2010).

1.note: Supports same-sex marriage/LGBT rights; opposed DOMA. HARD.

4.note: Backed ENDA and DADT repeal, 'strong supporter of LGBT rights' — contrary to opposing LGBTQ promotion in policy. HARD.

1.note: 'Don't send most illegal immigrants back; deport criminal immigrants, but don't send back others' — contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens. HARD (2010).

profile.confidence_note: Sitting NC Commissioner of Labor (R), in office since 2025-01-02. Unusually well-documented via his own iVoterGuide questionnaire (verbatim positions) plus official NCDOL actions and an NC Values Coalition endorsement. Strongly and consistently conservative across the rubric; no deviation.

luke-farley.notes_set: Web-verified sitting NC Commissioner of Labor (R), sworn in 2025-01-02; former workplace-safety attorney. DOCUMENTED via his iVoterGuide questionnaire (his own answers) and official acts: 'Life must be protected from conception to natural death,' 'I cannot support elective abortion under any circumstances,' opposes assisted suicide, 'not a single red cent' to abortion providers; 'Marriage is a God-ordained... union of one man and one woman,' opposes biological males in women's sports, protects children from 'gender ideology'; 'dollars should follow the child' (school choice/homeschool), written parental consent for any treatment of children; self-identifies as Christian, 'no legitimate reason for government ever to close churches,' businesses not forced to provide services violating religious belief; opposes ESG for public funds; would limit mail-in voting to excuse-only; pledges to challenge federal overreach; as Labor Commissioner enforces mandatory E-Verify; 'I support constitutional carry' and opposes infringing 2A rights. Ended DEI metrics at NCDOL (merit-based hiring). Endorsed by NC Values Coalition. Cite iVoterGuide for the questionnaire positions (NOT his campaign site).

0.note: 'Life must be protected from conception to natural death.' HARD.

1.note: 'I cannot support elective abortion under any circumstances' — total opposition, beyond mere restrictions. HARD.

3.note: Assisted suicide 'Strongly Disagree'; life protected to natural death. HARD.

4.note: Strong pro-life record, 'not a single red cent' to abortion providers, NC Values (pro-life) endorsement; no abortion-industry PAC ties found. HARD.

0.note: 'Marriage is a God-ordained, sacred and legal union of one man and one woman.' HARD.

1.note: 'The government is powerless to redefine it' — opposes redefinition of marriage. HARD.

2.note: 'Biological males should not be allowed to participate in women's sports'; protects children from 'gender ideology.' HARD.

4.note: Opposes promotion of gender ideology to children; affirms man-woman marriage in policy. HARD.

0.note: 'Dollars should follow the child, always' — public/private/homeschool. HARD.

1.note: Parents must give written consent before any medical treatment of children. HARD.

2.note: Children 'must be protected from... gender ideology, grooming' in education. HARD.

0.note: Openly professes Christian faith in public office ('a Christian, husband, father'); 'no legitimate reason for government ever to close churches.' HARD.

1.note: Businesses should not be required to provide services violating sincere religious beliefs; NC Values cites his support for religious freedom in the workplace. HARD.

4.note: Opposes ESG for public funds; 'maximizing return for the taxpayers.' HARD.

2.note: Mail-in voting should be limited to excuse-only categories (65+, sick/disabled, pregnant, out-of-county) — aligns with absentee-by-exception. HARD.

3.note: Calls the current mail-in system 'one of the main challenges to election integrity'; would restrict it. HARD.
Pledges to 'challenge federal overreach (e.g., PREP Act)' affecting workers; affirms 'small, constitutional government' / state authority. HARD.
As Labor Commissioner, NCDOL investigates all E-Verify complaints and he is 'committed to enforcing these laws' — documented support for mandatory E-Verify. HARD (official action).

1.note: 'Infringing on the 2A rights of law-abiding citizens does nothing to make us safer' — opposes gun-control infringements. HARD.

profile.confidence_note: Sitting NC Commissioner of Insurance / State Fire Marshal (R), in office since 2017, re-elected 2024. Documented positions come from his NC Family Policy Council 2024 survey responses (HARD). Solid social-issue record; no documented economic/election/federalism/immigration/2A positions; those nulled.

mike-causey.notes_set: Web-verified sitting NC Commissioner of Insurance & State Fire Marshal (R), in office since 2017-01-01, re-elected 2024-11-05; Army veteran (former Military Police). DOCUMENTED via the NC Family Policy Council 2024 candidate survey (his own responses): supports a fetal-heartbeat abortion limit, born-alive protection, and no state funds to abortion providers; opposes euthanasia; opposes adding SOGI to nondiscrimination protected classes and opposes biological males in female sports; supports non-public-school scholarships (school choice), prohibiting K-4 gender/sexuality instruction, and parental consent for minors' care; says religious beliefs should influence public-office decisions and churches should keep tax-exempt status. Context: cooperated with the FBI (wore a wire) against a bribery scheme and in Dec 2025 urged President Trump not to pardon Greg Lindberg (anti-corruption, not a scored rubric position). No documented economic/sound-money, election-mechanism, federalism, immigration, or 2A positions — nulled.

3.note: Survey: opposes euthanasia / medical assistance in ending life ('NO'). HARD.

4.note: Pro-life survey record (heartbeat limit, born-alive, no state funds to abortion providers); no abortion-industry PAC ties found. HARD.

2.note: Survey: opposes biological males on female sports teams; opposes adding gender identity to protected classes — affirms biological sex. HARD.

4.note: Survey: opposes adding SOGI to nondiscrimination protected classes and opposes K-4 gender/sexuality instruction — opposes LGBTQ promotion in policy/schools. HARD.

0.note: Survey: supports state-funded scholarships for non-public-school parents (school choice). HARD.

1.note: Survey: opposes letting minors consent to treatment without parental consent — supports parental consent. HARD.

2.note: Survey: supports prohibiting K-4 instruction on gender identity/sexuality. HARD.

0.note: Survey: affirms religious beliefs should influence decisions in public office; supports churches keeping tax-exempt status. HARD.
